## 🚀 About This Project

> **The definitive Git & GitHub reference, built by developers, for developers. Zero fluff.**

Every command has its **own dedicated file** with a consistent, information-dense structure:

```
📌 What it does  →  🔧 Syntax  →  ✅ Examples (beginner → advanced)
📋 Comparison tables  →  ⚠️ Common mistakes  →  🔗 Related commands
```

Whether you're looking up a command fast, deciding between `merge` and `rebase`, or building your first CI/CD pipeline — this repo has you covered.

Browse the full site at **[git-github-commands.netlify.app](https://git-github-commands.netlify.app/)** — fully formatted, searchable, and mobile-ready.

---

## ⚡ Quick Access

> **Bookmark these first. They save the most time.**

| Resource | Best Used When... |
|:---------|:------------------|
| [📋 **CHEATSHEET.md**](./CHEATSHEET.md) | You need the right command fast — all 35+ on a single page |
| [⚖️ **COMPARISONS.md**](./COMPARISONS.md) | You're deciding between `merge vs rebase`, `reset vs revert`, `fetch vs pull` |
| [🔄 **WORKFLOWS.md**](./WORKFLOWS.md) | You need a step-by-step real-world scenario guide |
| [📖 **GLOSSARY.md**](./GLOSSARY.md) | A Git term is confusing you — 50+ terms explained plainly |
| [🗂️ **Git Overview**](./git/overview.MD) | You want to understand Git's internals first |
| [🌐 **Live Website**](https://git-github-commands.netlify.app/) | You're on mobile or prefer a cleaner reading experience |

---

## 📂 Repository Structure

<details>
<summary><b>🗂️ Click to view full folder tree</b></summary>

<br/>

```
Git-Github-Commands/
│
├── 📁 git/                                    # 35+ Git command files
│   ├── overview.MD                            # Git internals, architecture & mental models
│   │
│   ├── 📁 01-setup/                           ← Start here
│   │   ├── _index.md
│   │   ├── config.md                          # Username, email, editor, aliases
│   │   ├── init.md                            # Initialize a repository
│   │   ├── clone.md                           # Copy remote repo locally
│   │   └── help.md                            # Get help for any command
│   │
│   ├── 📁 02-staging-and-committing/          ← Daily workflow
│   │   ├── _index.md
│   │   ├── add.md
│   │   ├── commit.md
│   │   ├── status.md
│   │   ├── diff.md
│   │   ├── mv.md
│   │   └── rm.md
│   │
│   ├── 📁 03-branching-and-merging/           ← Team collaboration
│   │   ├── _index.md
│   │   ├── branch.md
│   │   ├── checkout.md
│   │   ├── switch.md
│   │   ├── merge.md
│   │   ├── rebase.md
│   │   ├── cherry-pick.md
│   │   └── tag.md
│   │
│   ├── 📁 04-remote-workflow/                 ← Push, pull, fetch
│   │   ├── _index.md
│   │   ├── remote.md
│   │   ├── push.md
│   │   ├── pull.md
│   │   ├── fetch.md
│   │   └── submodule.md
│   │
│   ├── 📁 05-undoing-changes/                 ← Recovering from mistakes
│   │   ├── _index.md
│   │   ├── reset.md
│   │   ├── revert.md
│   │   ├── restore.md
│   │   ├── stash.md
│   │   └── clean.md
│   │
│   ├── 📁 06-inspection-and-history/          ← Debugging & auditing
│   │   ├── _index.md
│   │   ├── log.md
│   │   ├── show.md
│   │   ├── blame.md
│   │   ├── bisect.md
│   │   └── shortlog.md
│   │
│   ├── 📁 07-advanced/                        ← Power user tools
│   │   ├── _index.md
│   │   ├── reflog.md
│   │   ├── worktree.md
│   │   ├── archive.md
│   │   ├── gc.md
│   │   └── hooks.md
│   │
│   └── 📁 08-patching/                        ← Email-based workflows
│       ├── _index.md
│       ├── apply.md
│       ├── format-patch.md
│       └── am.md
│
├── 📁 github/                                 # GitHub features & CLI
│   ├── overview.md
│   ├── 📁 01-github-basics/
│   │   ├── fork.md · pull-request.md · issues.md · star-and-watch.md
│   ├── 📁 02-github-cli/
│   │   ├── gh-auth.md · gh-repo.md · gh-pr.md · gh-issus.md · gh-gist.md
│   └── 📁 03-github-actions/
│       ├── workflow-syntax.md · common-workflows.md
│
├── 📋 CHEATSHEET.md                           # All commands, one page
├── ⚖️  COMPARISONS.md                         # 10 command comparisons
├── 🔄 WORKFLOWS.md                            # 10 real-world scenarios
├── 📖 GLOSSARY.md                             # 50+ Git terms explained
├── 🤝 CONTRIBUTING.md
├── index.html · script.js · style.css        # Live website source
└── README.md
```

</details>

---

<div align="center">
<img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," width="100%"/>
</div>

<br/>

### 📁 01 — Setup

> Configure Git and create your first repository. Start here if you're new, or revisit to add aliases and advanced config.

| Command | What it does |
|:--------|:-------------|
| [`git config`](./git/01-setup/config.md) | Set your name, email, default editor, and create time-saving aliases |
| [`git init`](./git/01-setup/init.md) | Initialize a new local repository in the current directory |
| [`git clone`](./git/01-setup/clone.md) | Copy a remote repository to your local machine |
| [`git help`](./git/01-setup/help.md) | Get documentation for any Git command, right in the terminal |

→ **[View the 01-setup folder](./git/01-setup/)**

---

### 📁 02 — Staging & Committing

> The core loop every developer runs every single day. Learn to stage selectively, write clean commits, and inspect changes before you save them.

| Command | What it does |
|:--------|:-------------|
| [`git add`](./git/02-staging-and-committing/add.md) | Stage changes — whole files, specific lines, or interactive hunks |
| [`git commit`](./git/02-staging-and-committing/commit.md) | Save a permanent snapshot of staged changes to project history |
| [`git status`](./git/02-staging-and-committing/status.md) | Check what's staged, unstaged, and untracked in your working tree |
| [`git diff`](./git/02-staging-and-committing/diff.md) | See exact line-by-line changes before committing |
| [`git mv`](./git/02-staging-and-committing/mv.md) | Move or rename tracked files while preserving history |
| [`git rm`](./git/02-staging-and-committing/rm.md) | Remove files from Git tracking (and optionally from disk) |

→ **[View the 02-staging-and-committing folder](./git/02-staging-and-committing/)**

---

### 📁 03 — Branching & Merging

> Where real collaboration happens. Understand branching deeply — including when to merge vs rebase, and how to apply individual commits across branches.

| Command | What it does |
|:--------|:-------------|
| [`git branch`](./git/03-branching-and-merging/branch.md) | Create, list, rename, and delete branches |
| [`git checkout`](./git/03-branching-and-merging/checkout.md) | Switch branches or restore files (the classic multi-purpose command) |
| [`git switch`](./git/03-branching-and-merging/switch.md) | The modern, focused way to switch branches — recommended over checkout |
| [`git merge`](./git/03-branching-and-merging/merge.md) | Combine two branches together, preserving full history |
| [`git rebase`](./git/03-branching-and-merging/rebase.md) | Rewrite commit history for a clean, linear timeline |
| [`git cherry-pick`](./git/03-branching-and-merging/cherry-pick.md) | Apply one specific commit from any branch onto another |
| [`git tag`](./git/03-branching-and-merging/tag.md) | Mark release versions (`v1.0.0`, `v2.3.1`, etc.) permanently |

→ **[View the 03-branching-and-merging folder](./git/03-branching-and-merging/)**

---

### 📁 04 — Remote Workflows

> How your local code connects to the world. Push, pull, fetch, and manage remotes confidently.

| Command | What it does |
|:--------|:-------------|
| [`git remote`](./git/04-remote-workflow/remote.md) | Add, view, rename, and remove remote connections |
| [`git push`](./git/04-remote-workflow/push.md) | Upload local commits to a remote repository |
| [`git pull`](./git/04-remote-workflow/pull.md) | Download and automatically merge remote changes |
| [`git fetch`](./git/04-remote-workflow/fetch.md) | Download remote changes without merging — safe to inspect first |
| [`git submodule`](./git/04-remote-workflow/submodule.md) | Manage nested Git repositories inside your project |

→ **[View the 04-remote-workflow folder](./git/04-remote-workflow/)**

---

### 📁 05 — Undoing Changes

> Everyone breaks things. This is the most important section — know these before you need them, not after.

| Command | What it does |
|:--------|:-------------|
| [`git reset`](./git/05-undoing-changes/reset.md) | Undo commits or unstage files — rewrites history, use with care on shared branches |
| [`git revert`](./git/05-undoing-changes/revert.md) | Safely undo a pushed commit by creating a new undo-commit |
| [`git restore`](./git/05-undoing-changes/restore.md) | Discard working directory changes without touching history |
| [`git stash`](./git/05-undoing-changes/stash.md) | Temporarily shelve uncommitted work so you can switch context cleanly |
| [`git clean`](./git/05-undoing-changes/clean.md) | Remove untracked files and directories from your working tree |

→ **[View the 05-undoing-changes folder](./git/05-undoing-changes/)**

---

### 📁 06 — Inspection & History

> Understand exactly what happened, when, and who did it. These are your debugging and auditing tools.

| Command | What it does |
|:--------|:-------------|
| [`git log`](./git/06-inspection-and-history/log.md) | View and filter commit history with powerful formatting options |
| [`git show`](./git/06-inspection-and-history/show.md) | Inspect a specific commit's full changes and metadata |
| [`git blame`](./git/06-inspection-and-history/blame.md) | See who last modified each line of a file and in which commit |
| [`git bisect`](./git/06-inspection-and-history/bisect.md) | Binary search through history to pinpoint exactly when a bug was introduced |
| [`git shortlog`](./git/06-inspection-and-history/shortlog.md) | Summarize commit history by author — great for release changelogs |

→ **[View the 06-inspection-and-history folder](./git/06-inspection-and-history/)**

---

### 📁 07 — Advanced

> Power tools for power users. Most developers never explore these — the ones who do gain a real, lasting edge.

| Command | What it does |
|:--------|:-------------|
| [`git reflog`](./git/07-advanced/reflog.md) | Recover "lost" commits — your ultimate safety net after anything goes wrong |
| [`git worktree`](./git/07-advanced/worktree.md) | Check out multiple branches simultaneously in separate directories |
| [`git archive`](./git/07-advanced/archive.md) | Export a snapshot of the repo as `.zip` or `.tar.gz` |
| [`git gc`](./git/07-advanced/gc.md) | Run garbage collection to clean up and optimize the repository database |
| [`git hooks`](./git/07-advanced/hooks.md) | Automate custom scripts triggered by Git events (pre-commit, post-merge, etc.) |

→ **[View the 07-advanced folder](./git/07-advanced/)**

---

### 📁 08 — Patching

> File-based and email-based patch workflows. Essential for open-source projects and kernel-style contribution.

| Command | What it does |
|:--------|:-------------|
| [`git apply`](./git/08-patching/apply.md) | Apply changes from a `.patch` file to your working tree |
| [`git format-patch`](./git/08-patching/format-patch.md) | Create `.patch` files from commits, ready to email or share |
| [`git am`](./git/08-patching/am.md) | Apply a mailbox patch file, preserving the original author's attribution |

→ **[View the 08-patching folder](./git/08-patching/)**

---

<div align="center">
<img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," width="100%"/>
</div>

<br/>

### 📁 01 — GitHub Basics

> The platform features every developer encounters on a daily basis.

| Topic | Description |
|:------|:------------|
| [🍴 Fork](./github/01-github-basics/fork.md) | Copy someone's repository to your account to propose changes |
| [🔀 Pull Request](./github/01-github-basics/pull-request.md) | Propose, review, discuss, and merge code changes |
| [🐛 Issues](./github/01-github-basics/issues.md) | Track bugs, features, and open-ended project discussions |
| [⭐ Stars & Watch](./github/01-github-basics/star-and-watch.md) | Bookmark repos and manage your notification feed |

→ **[View the 01-github-basics folder](./github/01-github-basics/)**

---

### 📁 02 — GitHub CLI

> Do everything GitHub can do — right from your terminal. Never leave your editor.

| Command | Description |
|:--------|:------------|
| [`gh auth`](./github/02-github-cli/gh-auth.md) | Authenticate with GitHub and manage credentials |
| [`gh repo`](./github/02-github-cli/gh-repo.md) | Create, clone, fork, and manage repos from the terminal |
| [`gh pr`](./github/02-github-cli/gh-pr.md) | Create, review, and merge Pull Requests without leaving the CLI |
| [`gh issue`](./github/02-github-cli/gh-issus.md) | Create, assign, label, and close Issues from your terminal |
| [`gh gist`](./github/02-github-cli/gh-gist.md) | Create and share code snippets via GitHub Gists |

→ **[View the 02-github-cli folder](./github/02-github-cli/)**

---

### 📁 03 — GitHub Actions

> Automate everything — tests, deployments, releases, code quality. Includes **8 battle-tested workflow templates** ready to copy into your project.

| Topic | Description |
|:------|:------------|
| [📝 Workflow Syntax](./github/03-github-actions/workflow-syntax.md) | Complete YAML syntax reference — triggers, jobs, steps, secrets, expressions |
| [⚙️ Common Workflows](./github/03-github-actions/common-workflows.md) | 8 production-ready copy-paste workflows for real projects |

→ **[View the 03-github-actions folder](./github/03-github-actions/)**

---

## 🗺️ Learning Roadmap

<div align="center">

<img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," width="100%"/>

</div>

| Stage | Steps | Focus |
|:------|:------|:-------|
| 🔰 **Beginner** | Overview → Setup → Staging → Branching | Build the daily workflow |
| 📈 **Intermediate** | Remote → Comparisons → Workflows → GitHub | Collaborate with teams |
| 🚀 **Advanced** | Undoing → Advanced Tools → CLI → Actions | Master the full ecosystem |

---

## 🛠️ How To Use

**Option 1 — Browse the Live Website** *(recommended, zero setup)*

```
https://git-github-commands.netlify.app/
```

**Option 2 — Clone Locally**

```bash
git clone https://github.com/Ankit-Tank/Git-Github-Commands.git
cd Git-Github-Commands
```

**Quick Lookup Guide**

```bash
# Need a command fast?          →  open CHEATSHEET.md
# Comparing two similar commands? →  open COMPARISONS.md
# Need a real-world guide?      →  open WORKFLOWS.md
# Term is confusing you?        →  open GLOSSARY.md
# Starting from scratch?        →  open git/overview.MD
```

Each subfolder has a `_index.md` that maps every command in that section, shows how they relate, and links you to the right file.

---

## 🤝 Contributing

Found a mistake? Want to improve an explanation or add a missing command? Contributions are welcome.

```bash
# 1. Fork this repository on GitHub

# 2. Create a branch for your change
git switch -c docs/improve-rebase-explanation

# 3. Make changes following the existing file template

# 4. Commit with a clear conventional message
git commit -m "docs: add visual diagram to git rebase"

# 5. Push and open a Pull Request
git push origin docs/improve-rebase-explanation
```

See **[CONTRIBUTING.md](./CONTRIBUTING.md)** for the full file template, style guide, and review process.

---

## 📊 Everything at a Glance

<div align="center">

| Category | Commands & Topics |
|:---------|:-----------------|
| ⚙️ **Setup** | `config` · `init` · `clone` · `help` |
| 📦 **Staging** | `add` · `commit` · `status` · `diff` · `mv` · `rm` |
| 🌿 **Branching** | `branch` · `checkout` · `switch` · `merge` · `rebase` · `cherry-pick` · `tag` |
| 🌐 **Remote** | `remote` · `push` · `pull` · `fetch` · `submodule` |
| ↩️ **Undoing** | `reset` · `revert` · `restore` · `stash` · `clean` |
| 🔍 **Inspection** | `log` · `show` · `blame` · `bisect` · `shortlog` |
| ⚡ **Advanced** | `reflog` · `worktree` · `archive` · `gc` · `hooks` |
| 🩹 **Patching** | `apply` · `format-patch` · `am` |
| 🐙 **GitHub** | Fork · PR · Issues · Stars · Profile · CLI · Actions |

</div>
